title: Sentence Expansion — Note Form to Full Clinical Sentences
brief: Expand each note-form line into a complete, professional sentence. Replace abbreviations, add subject + verb, and use full words for durations.

targets.noteForm: Pt c/o cough x 3/52, productive, no haemoptysis.
targets.exemplar: The patient reports a productive cough that has persisted for three weeks, with no associated haemoptysis.
targets.rationale: Convert 'c/o' and '3/52' to full words; add subject + verb.

targets.noteForm: BP 172/98, HR 88 reg, sats 96% RA, afebrile.
targets.exemplar: On examination, his blood pressure was 172/98 mmHg with a regular pulse of 88 beats per minute, oxygen saturations of 96% on room air, and he was afebrile.
targets.rationale: Spell out 'blood pressure' and 'room air'; introduce with 'On examination'.

targets.noteForm: Tx: started ramipril 2.5 mg OD, lifestyle advice given.
targets.exemplar: He was commenced on ramipril 2.5 mg once daily, and lifestyle advice was provided regarding diet, exercise, and weight reduction.
targets.rationale: Replace 'Tx:' and 'OD'; use passive clinical voice.
